text: Among fresh skill-pack repos, I would watch phuryn/pm-skills and anthropics/skills first. One is strong packaging for repeated processes, the other is a clean official baseline for Claude-native workflows.
shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice is useful not because it contains everything, but because you can steal one or two hard rules about verification and session memory and work better immediately.

When the same thing shows up in several places, that is not noise anymore. Official sources and practitioners keep converging on the same themes: worktrees, verification-before-completion, layered context, and disciplined agent memory.

If I had to explain the difference between official and practitioner layers quickly, I would place openai/skills, anthropics/skills, and shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice side by side. That makes capability truth and practice truth obvious.
